brief_justification: The only overlap is the word 'collisions': paper A is nonperturbative QFT particle production, modelling ultra-relativistic bubble-wall collisions as a partonic on-shell scattering of the quanta in Lorentz-contracted walls, whereas paper B is a microlocal/spectral-theory result characterizing semiclassical measures of Coulomb-operator eigenfunctions as Kepler-flow-invariant measures via Moser regularization of the Coulomb (origin) collision. No shared governing equation, threshold, or branching/scattering structure maps role-for-role between them, so the machinery differs.
